primaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Severity of PTSD Symptoms Measured by Clinician Administered PTSD Scale for DSM-IV (CAPS-DX)
primaryOutcomes.description: Scores range from 0-70, higher scores represent more severe symptoms

eligibilityCriteria.description: DSM-IV diagnosis of PTSD or meets the five of the six diagnostic criteria for PTSD (event, hyperarousal, re-experiencing, duration, and distress/impaired functioning symptom criteria), but not the avoidance/numbing symptom criteria
Concurrent use of daily benzodiazepine; daily use of antidepressant medication allowed if dose has been stable for the 3 months before study entry
Exposure therapy or additional cognitive therapy during the course of the study (supportive psychotherapy is allowed if ongoing for at least 3 months before study entry)
Current use of beta blockers, amiodarone, chlorpromazine, cimetidine, clonidine, or digoxin
